title: LEE due to mass range [25-30] GeV in dimuon excess analysis SUS-23-011

posts.name: Alexandre Nikitenko
posts.text:
Dear experts,
with feldman-cousins method and 300M toys we confirmed a local significance of 4.7 (p-value = 1.3e-6) evaluated with analytical formulae by Vitelles with 3 independent signal strengths (for 6 search regions).
However, when we run feldman-cousins for evaluation global significance due to only the mass range [25-30] GeV (we fixed width to the best fit value of 3.2 GeV) we got definitely wrong p-value =
posts.cooked_html:
Dear experts,

with feldman-cousins method and 300M toys we confirmed a local significance of 4.7 (p-value = 1.3e-6) evaluated with analytical formulae by Vitelles with 3 independent signal strengths (for 6 search regions).

However, when we run feldman-cousins for evaluation global significance due to only the mass range [25-30] GeV (we fixed width to the best fit value of 3.2 GeV) we got definitely wrong p-value =

posts.name: Alexandre Nikitenko
posts.text:
… continue,
wrong p-value = 0.0145 +/- 0.0011954 , corresponding to  significance 2.2.
I think it is wrong since in Higgs discovery paper with a local significance 5.0,
the global significance in the mass range of 115-130 GeV (15 GeV range) of
4.6.    The local significances are similar 5.0 vs 4.7 and our mass range is 5 GeV which is 3 times smaller.  We can’t get global significance of 2.2 I think.  In Higgs analysis they had 5 channels and each channel had several event categories.
So, we think feldman-cousins give a wrong number for LEE in our case.
Any comment are very welcome. In the meantime we will build our machinery to evaluate LEE.
We run command:
combine -d combined.txt -M HybridNew --LHCmode LHC-feldman-cousins --clsAcc 0 --singlePoint mu_sr1_2017=0,mu_sr2_2017=0,mu_sr3_2017=0 --saveToys --saveHybridResult -T 10000 -s -1 --setParameters width=3.2 --freezeParameters width,sigma,r --setParameterRanges mean=25.0,30.0:mu_sr1_2017=0.0,100.0:mu_sr2_2017=0.0,100.0:mu_sr3_2017=0.0,150.0 --redefineSignalPOI mu_sr1_2017,mu_sr2_2017,mu_sr3_2017 -n run_1
Cheers, Sasha.
posts.cooked_html:
… continue,

wrong p-value = 0.0145 +/- 0.0011954 , corresponding to significance 2.2.

I think it is wrong since in Higgs discovery paper with a local significance 5.0,
the global significance in the mass range of 115-130 GeV (15 GeV range) of
4.6. The local significances are similar 5.0 vs 4.7 and our mass range is 5 GeV which is 3 times smaller. We can’t get global significance of 2.2 I think. In Higgs analysis they had 5 channels and each channel had several event categories.

So, we think feldman-cousins give a wrong number for LEE in our case.

Any comment are very welcome. In the meantime we will build our machinery to evaluate LEE.

We run command:

combine -d combined.txt -M HybridNew --LHCmode LHC-feldman-cousins --clsAcc 0 --singlePoint mu_sr1_2017=0,mu_sr2_2017=0,mu_sr3_2017=0 --saveToys --saveHybridResult -T 10000 -s -1 --setParameters width=3.2 --freezeParameters width,sigma,r --setParameterRanges mean=25.0,30.0:mu_sr1_2017=0.0,100.0:mu_sr2_2017=0.0,100.0:mu_sr3_2017=0.0,150.0 --redefineSignalPOI mu_sr1_2017,mu_sr2_2017,mu_sr3_2017 -n run_1

Cheers, Sasha.
